Off Calendar Aug-11-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS OLIVERI LAW, LLP, and MATTHEW OLIVERI's Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' "motion for sanctions pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 128.7" is denied without prejudice.

"A notice of motion must state in the opening paragraph the nature of the order being sought and the grounds for issuance of the order." (CRC 3.1110(a).) Here, defendants' notice states no grounds, just unspecified "violations of Code of Civil Procedure Section 128.7." CCP 128.7 is a lengthy statute that can be violated in many ways and defendants' papers make many allegations. The court cannot be expected to sift through the papers to speculate what the grounds for defendants' motion might be.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JAYNRY W. MAK, Esq. (Tel: 415-307-9783) for the plaintiff;
Atty. MATTHEW M. OLIVERI, Esq. of Oliveri Law, LLP (Tel: 925-553-3371) as pro per defendant and for defendant Oliveri Law, LLP.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS OLIVERI LAW, LLP AND MATTHEW OLIVERI'S Motion To Set Aside Default.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Oliveri Law, LLC's motion to set aside default is granted for improper service of summons. (Code Civ. Proc., section 473, subd. (d).) Plaintiff argues substitute process was proper pursuant to C.C.P. section 415.20. However, when serving a corporate entity, the papers must be delivered to a person who is apparently in charge of such office, such as the personal secretary of the person to be served, and such delivery must be made during the usual office hours. (Code Civ. Proc., section 415.20, subd. (a).) It is undisputed that the service of summons and related papers were served on attorney George Pfeiffer in November 2020. (Opp. 5:20-22; Oliveri Decl., 10; Pfeiffer Decl. 6-7.) George Pfeiffer is a partner in Pfeiffer Law that rents office spaces in the same suite as Oliveri Law. (Pfeiffer Decl., 3.) Four law firms share a "bullpen" area where not one single office manager oversees all four law firms. (Id. 4.) Pfeiffer told the process server he was not affiliated with Oliveri Law and was not designated to accept service. (Id. 7-9.) Thus, the default against Oliveri Law, LLC is set aside. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied.

Defendant to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

The matter is called. ARGUMENT is held. At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendants Oliveri Law, LLP and Matthew Oliveri's demurrer is ordered off calendar for failure to comply with CCP 430.41 and because the former is in default. Default was entered against Oliveri Law, LLP on January 15, 2021, which foreclosures any demurrer by it. (Devlin v. Kearny Mesa AMC/Jeep/Renault, Inc. (1984) 155 Cal.App.3d 381, 385.) However, counsel's declaration does not say that issue was addressed in a meet and confer. (See CCP 430.41(a).) Moreover, defendants' reply appears to argue that the default was improperly entered, but the register of actions reveals no motion to that effect.

Order signed.
